flashcards.front: What is the key property of a BST?
flashcards.back: All nodes in left subtree are less than root, all in right subtree are greater.

flashcards.front: What is the time complexity of BST operations in balanced trees?
flashcards.back: O(log n) due to reduced height.

flashcards.front: What traversal gives sorted order in BST?
What is the worst-case height of a BST?
flashcards.back: O(n) when tree becomes skewed like linked list.

flashcards.front: How are duplicates typically handled in BST?
flashcards.back: Either store in left subtree or use counter in node.

flashcards.front: What is successor in BST deletion?
flashcards.back: Smallest node in right subtree when deleting node with two children.

flashcards.front: What makes AVL trees different from basic BST?
flashcards.back: AVL maintains balance using height difference constraints.
BST with pointers to inorder predecessor/successor for efficient traversal.
Recursively compare with current node, insert at null child position.

flashcards.front: What is the space complexity of BST?
Why is balancing important in BST?
flashcards.back: Prevents degeneration to linear structure maintaining O(log n) operations.

flashcards.front: What is the minimum number of nodes in height h BST?
